name: Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2)
description: Hike the challenging Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2) for 6.6 miles, gaining 2405 feet, and enjoy panoramic views of the Alicante region.
region_intro: Hiking around Busot offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Cabeçó d'Or mountain, providing panoramic views of the Alicante region. The area is characterized by rugged mountain ranges, dense pine woods, and Mediterranean forest landscapes. Notable natural features include the impressive Canelobre Caves and the varied terrain of the Sierra Cabeçó d'Or. Trails range from gentle paths to challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Busot The most popular hiking route is Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2), a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 43 minutes to complete, offering expansive vistas from the mountain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruins at Collado del Polset loop from Cova del Canelobre, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path that explores historical ruins and natural features near the Canelobre Caves. Local hikers also love the Cabeçó d'Or Summit – Paso Hilary loop from Cova del Canelobre, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Busot is defined by mountain ascents, cave systems, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and difficult climbs, suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Busot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 210 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Busot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Busot?
A: Busot offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the rugged mountain ranges, dense pine woods, and Mediterranean forest landscapes at the foot of the Cabeçó d'Or mountain.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Busot?
A: The trails around Busot range from easy walks to challenging ascents. You'll find options for beginners, moderate excursions, and difficult climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868646479/cabeco-d-or-loop-trail-pr-cv-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2)&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31410552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Penya d'Home loop from Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt; offers an easier experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Busot?
A: Busot is suitable for hiking for much of the year, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter hiking is also possible, especially on lower elevation trails, though higher mountain routes might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Busot?
A: Yes, Busot has trails suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always available, routes with lower difficulty and shorter distances, such as some sections around the Canelobre Caves or the easier loops from Penya d'Home, can be enjoyable for families. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Busot?
A: Many trails in the Busot region are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry water for them. Specific rules may apply in certain natural parks or reserves, so check local signage.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Busot?
A: Yes, Busot offers several excellent circular hiking routes. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868646479/cabeco-d-or-loop-trail-pr-cv-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14630224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins at Collado del Polset loop from Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10323273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Summit – Paso Hilary loop from Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt;, all providing varied scenery and returning to your starting point.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Busot?
A: Busot is rich in natural attractions. The most prominent are the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3576092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, and the renowned Canelobre Caves, known for their vast interior and unique formations. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1040122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Illeta dels Banyets&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Les Salines ravine&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, the Busot area offers historical points of interest. The ruins of Busot Castle, an old Moorish fortress, provide fantastic views and a glimpse into the region's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14630224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins at Collado del Polset loop from Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores historical ruins near the Canelobre Caves.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Busot?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and attractions like the Canelobre Caves. In Busot town, you can find public parking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting point for designated parking spaces, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Can I reach the hiking areas in Busot using public transport?
A: While Busot itself is accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads outside the town center might require a short taxi ride or a longer walk. Buses connect Busot with Alicante, but services to more remote hiking starting points are limited. Planning ahead is recommended if relying solely on public transport.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Busot?
A: The trails in Busot are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 210 reviews. Hikers often praise the spectacular panoramic views from the Cabeçó d'Or mountain, the unique experience of combining hikes with a visit to the Canelobre Caves, and the diverse terrain that keeps every outing interesting.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails around Busot?
A: Hiking in Busot offers breathtaking views. From the slopes and summit of Cabeçó d'Or, you can enjoy expansive vistas of the Alicante region, stretching towards the Mediterranean coast on clear days. Trails also provide scenic perspectives of rugged mountain ranges, dense pine forests, and the dramatic landscapes surrounding the Canelobre Caves.

Q: Are there any easy routes for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, there are easier options for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31410552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Penya d'Home loop from Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt; is an easy trail, and there are other gentle paths around the base of the mountains or near the town that provide pleasant walks without significant elevation gain.
tips: With its top at an altitude of 1,210 meters above sea level, the views it offers are simply wonderful. You will be able to have a 360 degree panoramic view of the entire interior of Alicante as well as towards the beautiful beaches of the Costa Blanca. A highly recommended place!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2)?
faqs.answer: The full loop trail is approximately 10.6 kilometers (6.6 miles) long and generally takes around 4 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how much time you spend enjoying the views or navigating challenging sections.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. It requires a good level of physical fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy hiking shoes. You'll encounter significant elevation gain, rocky sections, loose stones, and steep climbs. Some parts may even require easy scrambling, especially if you opt for the summit detour.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and demanding. You'll hike on dirt tracks, established paths, and some road sections. Expect rocky and eroded areas, loose stones, and steep ascents. The route also winds through pine forests and Mediterranean scrub, offering a diverse hiking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near the renowned Cuevas del Canelobre. There is usually parking available in this area, which serves as a convenient starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers simply impressive and continually changing scenery. Key highlights include the challenging Paso Hilary and the rewarding Cabeçó d'Or Summit. From the higher elevations and the summit, you'll get magnificent 360-degree panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea, Peñón de Ifach, Sierra de Aitana, Puig Campana, and the city of Alicante. On clear days, you might even spot Ibiza.

faqs.question: Is the Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Cabeçó d'Or area is part of the Natura 2000 Network and designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). While dogs are generally allowed on trails in Spain, it's advisable to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on the challenging terrain. Always check for specific local regulations before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a special permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike the Cabeçó d'Or Loop Trail (PR-CV 2). It is a public access trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous ascent more challenging. Winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions at higher elevations, so be prepared for varied weather.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no natural water sources available directly on the trail. It is crucial to carry sufficient water for the entire hike, especially given its length and difficulty.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily the Cabeçó d'Or trail itself, which is also known as PRV 2-Cabeçó d'Or. It also briefly intersects with sections of the senda Canal de l'Homenet and the pujada sud-est al Cabeçó d'Or.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone or GPS device. A physical map or downloaded komoot map is also recommended, as some signage can be deteriorated.
